template: Vouch for my brother in the upcoming council vote — your reputation will carry weight where mine cannot.
Speak at the Pinewood summit. They listen to those whose alignment runs clean.
Carry my proposal across the gate without opening the seal. My name on it would close the door before it opened.
Witness the tally at the third hall. Three witnesses are needed, and the third has not arrived.
Walk the procession beside me to the granaries. The crowd knows your face; mine they have stopped reading.
Plant a sapling at the southern grove, in my mother's name. I'm not allowed near that ground anymore.
Carry word to the goddess's emissary that her shrine still stands. She'll trust you where she'd doubt me.
Light the eastern lantern before dawn. The path it shows isn't for me to walk anymore, but it should be lit for whoever does.
Sit through the third silence. Most break before the second; the goddess hears those who hold the third.
Speak the eight refusals at the river. Your voice will reach further than you think.
Find the runoff source poisoning the Thornwood stream. I've watched it brown for a season.
The wolves have been thinning. Track the pack-leader and bring me what you find — I won't ask why.
The bees haven't returned to the third hive. Walk the meadow before frost and tell me what's missing.
Carry these saplings to the burnt ridge. They'll take where the others didn't because the soil knows your boots aren't the ones that started the fire.
The herd is grazing the wrong side of the ridge. I can't go up there. You can.
Speak the Sovereign's refusal at the western gate before sundown. Your debt makes the words land.
Stand in my place at the Refusal Archive. I cannot enter; you can.
Refuse the count at the Mass Raid threshold. The number you refuse is the number that remembers you.
Walk into the dome and hold your breath through the eighth glyph. The dome will know if you didn't.
Carry this sealed letter to the Concordia herself. Don't read it. The seal will know.
Bring me a token from beyond this district. I want to know the world is still wider than the gate.
Witness me train tomorrow at dawn. A worked move with no audience is a half-move.
Carry a message I've been afraid to send. The recipient will understand even if you don't.
Eat at my table once before the season turns. The conversation matters more than the food, but the food matters too.
Wear something I made for one day. I'll know if you did, and I'll forget the asking once I see it.
